---
title: "Hugging Face Transformers"
author:
  - name: ML+X Community

date: 2026-02-24
date-format: long
image: "../../../images/hugging-face.png"

categories:
  - Libraries
  - NLP
  - Deep learning
  - LLM
  - Transformers
  - PyTorch
  - Foundation models
  - Hugging Face
---
## About this resource

[Hugging Face Transformers](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers) is an open-source library that provides thousands of pre-trained models for natural language processing, computer vision, audio, and multimodal tasks. Developed by Hugging Face, it has become the de facto standard for working with transformer-based models, offering a unified API to download, fine-tune, and deploy models like BERT, GPT-2, Llama, Whisper, and many more.

#### Key features
- **Massive model hub**: Access to over 400,000 pre-trained models through the [Hugging Face Hub](https://huggingface.co/models), spanning text, image, audio, and multimodal tasks.
- **Pipeline API**: Simple high-level API for common tasks like text classification, summarization, translation, question answering, and image classification — often in just a few lines of code.
- **Fine-tuning support**: Built-in `Trainer` API for fine-tuning models on custom datasets with minimal boilerplate.
- **Multi-framework support**: Works with PyTorch, TensorFlow, and JAX, allowing researchers to use their preferred framework.

## Integration and compatibility
Hugging Face Transformers integrates tightly with the broader Hugging Face ecosystem and popular ML frameworks.

- **Frameworks supported**: PyTorch, TensorFlow, JAX
- **Ecosystem**: [Datasets](https://huggingface.co/docs/datasets), [Tokenizers](https://huggingface.co/docs/tokenizers), [Accelerate](https://huggingface.co/docs/accelerate), [PEFT](https://huggingface.co/docs/peft) (parameter-efficient fine-tuning)
- **Installation**: `pip install transformers`

## Use cases
- **Text generation and chatbots**: Load and run LLMs for text generation, summarization, and conversational AI.
- **Sentiment analysis and classification**: Fine-tune models on domain-specific text classification tasks.
- **Named entity recognition**: Extract entities from text using pre-trained NER models.
- **Speech recognition**: Use models like Whisper for automatic speech recognition.
- **Image classification**: Apply vision transformers (ViT) to image classification tasks.

## Tutorials and resources
#### Getting started
- [**Official quick tour**](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/quicktour): Introduction to pipelines, models, and tokenizers.
- [**Hugging Face course**](https://huggingface.co/learn/nlp-course): Free, comprehensive course covering the Transformers library and NLP concepts.

#### Related libraries & tools
- [**Datasets**](https://huggingface.co/docs/datasets): Companion library for accessing and processing datasets.
- [**Gradio**](https://gradio.app): Build interactive ML demos powered by Transformers models.
- [**PEFT**](https://huggingface.co/docs/peft): Parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods like LoRA for training large models on limited hardware.

## Questions?
If you have any lingering questions about this resource, feel free to post them on the [ML+X Nexus Q&A](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/ML-X-Nexus/discussions/categories/q-a) on GitHub.

## See also
- [**Model**: Llama (Meta)](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Toolbox/Models/Llama.html): Meta's open-weight LLM, widely used through Hugging Face Transformers.
- [**Model**: Whisper (OpenAI)](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Toolbox/Models/Whisper.html): OpenAI's speech recognition model, available via Hugging Face.
- [**Library**: spaCy](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Toolbox/Libraries/spaCy.html): Industrial-strength NLP library, complementary to Transformers for production pipelines.
